storylets.title: {=floor_camp_talk_mess_grumbles_title}Talk at the mess
storylets.setup: {=floor_camp_talk_mess_grumbles_setup}Talk at the mess: how much longer? The stew is thin, the bread is thinner, and every soldier has a theory about what the lord is waiting for.

storylets.title: {=floor_camp_talk_dice_rattle_title}Dice by the fires
storylets.setup: {=floor_camp_talk_dice_rattle_setup}Dice rattle by the fires. Half the watch is broke and the other half is pretending. A corporal watches with arms folded, saying nothing yet.

storylets.title: {=floor_camp_talk_old_campaign_title}An old campaign
storylets.setup: {=floor_camp_talk_old_campaign_setup}A veteran reminisces about a campaign twenty years gone. Most of the men he names are dead. He does not say it that way, but the count runs one direction.

options.tooltip: {=floor_camp_talk_old_campaign_tt}Hear the old man out. No effect.

storylets.title: {=floor_camp_talk_homesick_title}A letter home
storylets.setup: {=floor_camp_talk_homesick_setup}A young one writes home by firelight. Mostly about the weather, he says, because the rest would only worry her. He reads a line back, then crosses it out.

storylets.title: {=floor_camp_talk_war_songs_title}A song at the fires
storylets.setup: {=floor_camp_talk_war_songs_setup}Someone starts a song, low and rough. Others drift in one voice at a time. By the third verse half the fires have joined, and the sergeants do not stop it.

storylets.title: {=floor_camp_talk_sergeant_complaints_title}The sergeant grumbles
storylets.setup: {=floor_camp_talk_sergeant_complaints_setup}The sergeant grumbles about the lord's latest order. Not loudly, not where any officer can hear, but loud enough that the nearest tents take the meaning.
